# Feature Spec: Task State Management

**FEAT-ID**: FEAT-003  
**Plan**: plan_todo_app.md  
**Status**: In Progress

## Objective

Implement task completion toggling and deletion functionality—when a task is marked as complete, it is grayed out with strikethrough text, and a trash icon appears allowing permanent deletion.

## Context

Building on FEAT-002's task creation and rendering, this feature adds interactive state management:
- Click on a task to toggle its completion status
- Visual feedback: completed tasks are grayed out with strike-through
- Trash icon (🗑️) appears only for completed tasks
- Click trash icon to permanently delete the task
- Updates persist in the in-memory array and re-render the UI

This feature does NOT include localStorage persistence (FEAT-004) or animations (FEAT-004).

## Requirements

### Functional
- Click task text to toggle completion status
- Completed tasks: gray background, strikethrough text, lowered opacity
- Trash icon displays only for completed tasks
- Click trash icon to delete task permanently
- Deletion removes task from array and updates UI
- Cursor changes to pointer on interactive elements

### Non-Functional
- Clear visual distinction between active and completed tasks
- Intuitive interaction (click task to complete, click trash to delete)
- Accessible button elements for trash icon
- Proper event delegation for dynamically added elements

### Design Specifications

**Completed Task Styles:**
- Background: #f0f0f0 (lighter gray)
- Text: #9e9e9e with text-decoration: line-through
- Opacity: 0.7

**Trash Icon:**
- Unicode symbol: 🗑️
- Color: #f44336 (red, for destructive action)
- Only visible on completed tasks
- Hover effect: slightly darker red

## Tasks

### TASK-001: Update Task Data Structure with Toggle Function

**Implementation:**

1. **Toggle Task Function:**
   ```javascript
   /**
    * Toggles the completion status of a task
    * @param {string} taskId - The ID of the task to toggle
    */
   function toggleTask(taskId) {
     const task = todos.find(t => t.id === taskId);
     if (task) {
       task.completed = !task.completed;
       renderTasks(); // Re-render to show updated state
     }
   }
   ```

2. **Delete Task Function:**
   ```javascript
   /**
    * Deletes a task permanently
    * @param {string} taskId - The ID of the task to delete
    */
   function deleteTask(taskId) {
     todos = todos.filter(t => t.id !== taskId);
     renderTasks(); // Re-render to remove deleted task
   }
   ```

**Unit Tests:**
Manual verification (console testing):
- [ ] Create task, call `toggleTask(id)`, verify completed flag changes
- [ ] Call `toggleTask(id)` again, verify it toggles back
- [ ] Create task, call `deleteTask(id)`, verify task removed from array
- [ ] Verify todos array length decreases after deletion

**Documentation Update:**
Add JSDoc comments (included above):
- Explain toggle behavior (flips boolean)
- Explain delete behavior (filters array)
- Note that both trigger re-render

---

### TASK-002: Update createTaskElement to Handle Completion State

**Implementation:**

Update the `createTaskElement` function to include:

1. **Apply completion class if task is completed:**
   ```javascript
   function createTaskElement(task) {
     const li = document.createElement('li');
     li.className = 'todo-item';
     if (task.completed) {
       li.classList.add('completed');
     }
     li.dataset.id = task.id;
     
     // Task text span with click handler for toggling
     const textSpan = document.createElement('span');
     textSpan.className = 'todo-text';
     textSpan.textContent = task.text;
     textSpan.style.cursor = 'pointer';
     textSpan.addEventListener('click', () => toggleTask(task.id));
     
     li.appendChild(textSpan);
     
     // Add delete button only if task is completed
     if (task.completed) {
       const deleteBtn = document.createElement('button');
       deleteBtn.className = 'delete-btn';
       deleteBtn.textContent = '🗑️';
       deleteBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Delete task');
       deleteBtn.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
         e.stopPropagation(); // Prevent li click event
         deleteTask(task.id);
       });
       
       li.appendChild(deleteBtn);
     }
     
     return li;
   }
   ```

**Unit Tests:**
Manual verification:
- [ ] Create task, toggle it, verify 'completed' class applied
- [ ] Verify trash button appears only when task is completed
- [ ] Click task text, verify toggle works
- [ ] Click trash icon, verify task is deleted
- [ ] Verify event.stopPropagation prevents unintended behavior

**Documentation Update:**
Add comments explaining:
- Conditional 'completed' class application
- Click event on text span for toggling
- Conditional rendering of delete button
- stopPropagation to prevent event bubbling

---

### TASK-003: Add CSS Styles for Completed Tasks and Delete Button

**Implementation:**
In the `<style>` section, add after the `.todo-text` rule:

```css
/* ===== COMPLETED TASK STYLES ===== */
/* Visual feedback for completed tasks: gray out with strikethrough */
.todo-item.completed {
  background: #f0f0f0;
  opacity: 0.7;
}

.todo-item.completed .todo-text {
  color: var(--color-accent);
  text-decoration: line-through;
}

/* ===== DELETE BUTTON STYLES ===== */
/* Trash icon button for completed tasks */
.delete-btn {
  background: none;
  border: none;
  font-size: 18px;
  cursor: pointer;
  padding: 4px 8px;
  color: #f44336;
  transition: color 0.2s;
  margin-left: var(--spacing-sm);
}

.delete-btn:hover {
  color: #d32f2f;
}

.delete-btn:focus {
  outline: 2px solid var(--color-accent);
  outline-offset: 2px;
}

/* ===== INTERACTIVE STYLES ===== */
/* Pointer cursor for clickable task text */
.todo-item:not(.completed) .todo-text {
  cursor: pointer;
}

.todo-item:not(.completed) .todo-text:hover {
  color: var(--color-text-secondary);
}
```

**Unit Tests:**
Manual verification:
- [ ] Completed tasks have gray background and strikethrough
- [ ] Completed tasks have reduced opacity (0.7)
- [ ] Trash button appears with red color
- [ ] Trash button hover state works (darker red)
- [ ] Task text has pointer cursor and hover effect
- [ ] Focus outline appears on delete button when tabbed

**Documentation Update:**
Add CSS comments explaining:
- Visual feedback for completed state
- Destructive action color (red) for delete button
- Hover and focus states for accessibility
- Interactive cursor styles

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Users can click task text to toggle completion status
- [ ] Completed tasks are visually distinct (gray background, strikethrough, lower opacity)
- [ ] Trash icon appears only on completed tasks
- [ ] Users can click trash icon to delete completed tasks
- [ ] Task count in array updates correctly after deletion
- [ ] All interactive elements have proper cursor styles
- [ ] Delete button has focus state for keyboard navigation
- [ ] All code documented with inline comments

## Testing Notes

Manual testing steps:
1. Open `index.html` in browser
2. Add a task
3. Click the task text — verify it grays out with strikethrough
4. Verify trash icon appears
5. Click trash icon — verify task is deleted
6. Click an uncompleted task to mark it done
7. Click a completed task to unmark it (trash should disappear)
8. Test keyboard navigation (Tab to delete button, Enter to trigger)
9. Add multiple tasks and test toggling/deleting various combinations

## Out of Scope

- LocalStorage persistence (FEAT-004)
- Animations and transitions (FEAT-004)
- Edit task functionality
- Task categories or priorities
- Undo deletion

## Dependencies

- FEAT-001 (HTML Structure & Base Styles) — complete
- FEAT-002 (Task Creation & Rendering) — complete

## Notes

- Using Unicode emoji 🗑️ for trash icon to avoid external icon library
- Red color (#f44336) chosen for delete button following Material Design conventions for destructive actions
- Event.stopPropagation() used on delete button to prevent triggering task toggle
- Completed class toggle approach allows for easy state visualization
- Strikethrough with gray color provides clear visual feedback for completed tasks
